attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.criterion_name: Deliverables: completeness, reproducibility, and correct application of rules
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open the workbook and ensure visible cell formulas are present (no hard-coded numbers), add explicit cell-range citations in allocation_note.md (sheet and cell references for priority formulas and breach flags), and confirm automated validation cells/conditional formatting are demonstrably present. Re-run verification with the spreadsheet parser available to confirm formula implementation and absence of external links.
rubric_item_scores.notes: allocation_note.md explicitly states the exact priority formula (travel_minutes * crossmatch_minutes - 0.6 * organ_quality_score + ischemia_margin_min), the hard-threshold definitions (travel>223, crossmatch>91, organ_quality<5.6), an escalation rule (breaching records ranked ahead), and a reproducible sensitivity table (+/-5% preserving order R4>R2>R3>R1). The normalized actual_outputs include record_results that match the allocation_note table (priority values, breach counts, rankings, and recommendations), supporting internal consistency. However, the workbook binary (cold_ischemia_risk.xlsx) could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so visible cell formulas and automated flag implementations could not be independently inspected.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: allocation_note.md explicitly states the exact priority formula (travel_minutes * crossmatch_minutes - 0.6 * organ_quality_score + ischemia_margin_min), the hard-threshold definitions (travel>223, crossmatch>91, organ_quality<5.6), an escalation rule (breaching records ranked ahead), and a reproducible sensitivity table (+/-5% preserving order R4>R2>R3>R1). The normalized actual_outputs include record_results that match the allocation_note table (priority values, breach counts, rankings, and recommendations), supporting internal consistency. However, the workbook binary (cold_ischemia_risk.xlsx) could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so visible cell formulas and automated flag implementations could not be independently inspected.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit spreadsheet cell-range citations in allocation_note.md (sheet name and cell/range references for inputs, formula cells, threshold-flag cells, and sensitivity tab). Ensure the xlsx contains visible formulas (not hard-coded numbers) and avoid password protection; enable parsing (e.g., provide environment-friendly formats or ensure openpyxl compatibility) so automated verification can confirm formulas and automated flags.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. allocation_note.md explicitly states the exact priority formula matching the rubric and documents threshold rules, identifies two breached records (R4, R2), shows they were marked needs_review and moved to the front of the queue, and includes a +/-5% sensitivity analysis with stability results. The deterministic precheck confirms artifact presence. However, the spreadsheet file could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable in this environment) so visible cell formulas and in-workbook cell-level citations could not be verified. allocation_note.md uses record-level citations to the embedded packet rather than explicit spreadsheet cell ranges, and the xlsx parsing error prevents verification that formulas are implemented as cell references (not hard-coded) and that threshold flags are automated rather than manual notes. The author did note an internal packet inconsistency (hard_threshold_record_count mismatch) and flagged records as needs_review appropriately.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. allocation_note.md explicitly states the exact priority formula matching the rubric and documents threshold rules, identifies two breached records (R4, R2), shows they were marked needs_review and moved to the front of the queue, and includes a +/-5% sensitivity analysis with stability results. The deterministic precheck confirms artifact presence. However, the spreadsheet file could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable in this environment) so visible cell formulas and in-workbook cell-level citations could not be verified. allocation_note.md uses record-level citations to the embedded packet rather than explicit spreadsheet cell ranges, and the xlsx parsing error prevents verification that formulas are implemented as cell references (not hard-coded) and that threshold flags are automated rather than manual notes. The author did note an internal packet inconsistency (hard_threshold_record_count mismatch) and flagged records as needs_review appropriately.
